rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the same substantive nit (derive scan targets from per-pipeline metadata like _dit_modules instead of hardcoding), echoing the spirit of dsocek's ground-truth suggestion, but X also correctly notes the PR's before/after validation (matching xuechendi's request/tthakkal's reply) and lands on an approve-level verdict consistent with the actual two LGTM approvals. Y omits the validation point entirely and outputs 'REQUEST CHANGES' despite labeling its own findings 'minor'/'nit', directly contradicting the actual approved/merged outcome, and its output is bloated with triplicated review text and raw diagnostic dumps that hurt readability without adding grounded content.
